summary: Polymerization shrinkage significantly affects bonding effectiveness; however, it does not solely cause microleakage which results from multiple interrelated issues including but not limited to mechanical fitment precision during restoration placement among others besides just chemical interactions post-curing process. The statement "microleakage occurs predominantly because of polymerization shrinkage" overstates its role compared to broader considerations impacting seal quality around dental fillings or crowns
